summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

A machine cuts metal rods to a target length of 50 cm with a tolerance of 0.3 cm. The quality condition is |x - 50| ≤ 0.3. What is the acceptable range of rod lengths?

|x - 50| ≤ 0.3 becomes -0.3 ≤ x - 50 ≤ 0.3. Add 50: 49.7 ≤ x ≤ 50.3 cm.

Two friends live at positions -3 and 7 on a number line (in miles). They want to meet at a point equidistant from both. The equation |x - (-3)| = |x - 7| gives the meeting point. What is x?

The midpoint of -3 and 7 is (-3 + 7)/2 = 4/2 = 2. At x = 2, the distance to -3 is 5 and the distance to 7 is 5.

A basketball player's free-throw percentage varies. Over several games, the percentage p satisfies |p - 75| ≤ 8. What is the range of the player's free-throw percentage?

|p - 75| ≤ 8 becomes -8 ≤ p - 75 ≤ 8. Add 75: 67 ≤ p ≤ 83, so the range is 67% to 83%.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: A food product must be stored at a temperature within 4°F of 38°F. The inequality |T - 38| ≤ 4 represents safe storage. A thermometer reads 43°F. Is the product safely stored?

|43 - 38| = |5| = 5, which is greater than 4. The temperature is outside the safe range.

Example: A basketball player's free-throw percentage varies. Over several games, the percentage p satisfies |p - 75| ≤ 8. What is the range of the player's free-throw percentage?

|p - 75| ≤ 8 becomes -8 ≤ p - 75 ≤ 8. Add 75: 67 ≤ p ≤ 83, so the range is 67% to 83%.
